intermediate_mechanisms: OL dysfunction and myelin abnormalities impair neural plasticity through loss of metabolic and trophic support to neurons.
OL-microglia and OL-astrocyte interactions link oligodendrocyte dysfunction to neuroinflammatory signaling in MDD.

nodes.description: Elevated inflammatory cytokines (IL-6, TNF-alpha, CRP) observed in depression. Inflammation may contribute to monoamine depletion and neuroplasticity deficits.

evidence.reference_title: Major depressive disorder: hypothesis, mechanism, prevention and treatment.
evidence.snippet: The currently widely accepted theories of MDD pathogenesis include the neurotransmitter and receptor hypothesis, hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal (HPA) axis hypothesis, cytokine hypothesis, neuroplasticity hypothesis and systemic influence hypothesis

evidence.reference_title: Role of Inflammatory Mechanisms in Major Depressive Disorder: From Etiology to Potential Pharmacological Targets.
evidence.snippet: The involvement of central and peripheral inflammation in the pathogenesis and prognosis of major depressive disorder (MDD) has been demonstrated. The increase of pro-inflammatory cytokines (interleukin (IL)-1β, IL-6, IL-18, and TNF-α) in individuals with depression may elicit neuroinflammatory processes and peripheral inflammation

evidence.reference_title: Role of Inflammatory Mechanisms in Major Depressive Disorder: From Etiology to Potential Pharmacological Targets.
evidence.snippet: mechanisms that, in turn, can contribute to gut microbiota dysbiosis. Together, neuroinflammation and gut dysbiosis induce alterations in tryptophan metabolism, culminating in decreased serotonin synthesis, impairments in neuroplasticity-related mechanisms, and glutamate-mediated excitotoxicity.

evidence.reference_title: A meta-analysis of cytokines in major depression.
evidence.snippet: This meta-analysis reports significantly higher concentrations of the proinflammatory cytokines TNF-alpha and IL-6 in depressed subjects compared with control subjects.

nodes.description: Reduced BDNF and impaired synaptic plasticity in prefrontal cortex and hippocampus. Successful treatments restore neuroplasticity.

evidence.reference_title: Major depressive disorder: hypothesis, mechanism, prevention and treatment.
evidence.snippet: The currently widely accepted theories of MDD pathogenesis include the neurotransmitter and receptor hypothesis, hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal (HPA) axis hypothesis, cytokine hypothesis, neuroplasticity hypothesis and systemic influence hypothesis

evidence.reference_title: Glutamatergic Modulators for Major Depression from Theory to Clinical Use.
evidence.snippet: This narrative review provides a high-level overview of glutamate signaling in synaptogenesis and neural plasticity and the implications of glutamate dysregulation in depression.

evidence.reference_title: Role of Inflammatory Mechanisms in Major Depressive Disorder: From Etiology to Potential Pharmacological Targets.
evidence.snippet: neuroinflammation and gut dysbiosis induce alterations in tryptophan metabolism, culminating in decreased serotonin synthesis, impairments in neuroplasticity-related mechanisms, and glutamate-mediated excitotoxicity.

nodes.description: Oligodendrocyte (OL) dysfunction and myelin abnormalities contribute to depression pathophysiology through multiple mechanisms: impaired myelination reduces conduction velocity and disrupts neural circuit function; loss of OL-derived metabolic support decreases neuronal energy supply; compromised OL-glial crosstalk with microglia and astrocytes amplifies neuroinflammation. These dysfunctions are associated with altered brain connectivity and emotional processing deficits, and may represent a targetable node for therapeutic remyelination strategies.

evidence.reference_title: Oligodendrocyte dysfunction in major depressive disorder: Mechanistic insights and emerging therapies.
evidence.snippet: Emerging evidence increasingly implicates oligodendrocyte (OL) dysfunction as a key pathophysiological mechanism in MDD, extending beyond their classical role in myelination to include critical contributions to neural plasticity, metabolic support, and circuit regulation.

evidence.reference_title: Oligodendrocyte dysfunction in major depressive disorder: Mechanistic insights and emerging therapies.
evidence.snippet: Such dysfunctions manifest primarily as myelin abnormalities and are closely associated with depression-related alterations in brain connectivity and emotional processing.

evidence.reference_title: Oligodendrocyte dysfunction in major depressive disorder: Mechanistic insights and emerging therapies.
evidence.snippet: with particular emphasis on their interactions with neurons, astrocytes, and microglia within the central nervous system
